title: Coordinates, Scalars, Vector2, Angles, and Units · Failure Triage
summary: A preserved game trace contains one plausible state, timing, input, vector, rendering, collision, ownership, audio, accessibility, packaging, or release defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in unit-labeled 2D geometry model and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Declare screen origin, axes, handedness, logical viewport, centers, bounds, and world-to-screen conversion.
Use Vector2 addition, subtraction, scaling, magnitude, squared distance, dot, cross, copy, and mutation with explicit meaning.
Derive facing vectors from a named zero-angle and degree convention and test clockwise screen appearance.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Inspect the failure record · Debug Coordinate Convention
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug Asteroids team handles case 2559 during an offline cross-platform release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile screen coordinates, Vector2 magnitude and direction, angle convention, and explicit motion units; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native display, device, audio, file, package, and operating-system effects; then cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Declare screen origin, axes, handedness, logical viewport, centers, bounds, and world-to-screen conversion.

Inspect the Coordinate Convention trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-motion-units-ai5zt3
# Competency: asteroids-motion-units.
# Case 8241: an offline cross-platform release rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity.
# Changed case: inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death.
def evidence_asteroids_motion_units_ai5zt3(x: float = 3.0, y: float = 4.0, pixels_per_second: float = 120.0, seconds: float = 0.25, zero_normalized: bool = False) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_ai5zt3 = "ai5zt3-3tz5ia-8241"
    assert evidence_variant_ai5zt3
    evidence_axes_ai5zt3 = (
        "playerai5zt3",
        "seed8241",
        "input3tz5ia",
        "frameai5zt38241",
        "state8241ai5zt3",
        "playtest3tz5ia8241",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_ai5zt3)) == 6
    magnitude = (x * x + y * y) ** 0.5
    if magnitude <= 0.0:
        return False, magnitude, "zero-vector"
    if pixels_per_second < 0.0 or seconds < 0.0:
        return False, seconds, "unit-domain"
    if zero_normalized:
        return False, magnitude, "zero-normalization"
    displacement = pixels_per_second * seconds
    assert abs(magnitude - 5.0) < 1e-9
    return True, displacement, "explicit-units"
